pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: **Zach outmaneuvered amateurs like Gunner and Devin; now he toys with Ghost Pepper's doomed squad.** Look, when you’ve played in a World Cup as I have, you can sniff out the talent, and let me tell you, Mustard Boy, aka Zach, has done his homework with Brazil. With the Selecao’s recent form, they’re set to obliterate Devin's hapless Haiti, leaving Ghost Pepper to wonder if he picked his teams based on FIFA video game ratings instead of real-world football. It’s almost laughable that Devin thought Haiti had a shot in this group; this is a joke. Brazil will coast past Devin's ragtag bunch as easily as I coasted by defenders in my day. Rafe, or "The Noisemaker," might be trailing only by a point, but picking three teams that didn’t even qualify shows he’s just here for the chaos. While Zach's leading with 13 points, Gayden, "The Backpass Assassin," needs to remember backward thinking doesn't win tournaments. Meanwhile, Gunner, languishing in last place, picked DR Congo, which is about as clueless as you can get. This is not how fantasy works, Bubba G; you need teams that actually perform. Zach remains the only manager in this wreck of a pool who grasps the beautiful game — everyone else should call it quits.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach Set to Solidify Lead Against Devin in All-Mustard Showdown
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy to face Ghost Pepper; condiment supremacy at stake.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's Strategy Meets Question as Mexico Topples Korea Republic
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Backpass Assassin questioned for losing to manager's teenage son.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner Poised for Redemption with Morocco Facing Untested Scotland
pundit_takes.subtitle: Bubba G banking on Tier 2 to climb from the basement.

pundit_takes.headline: USA's Ghost Pepper Ready to Sizzle Against Undrafted Australia
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin's spicy roster needs points to catch Mustard Boy's Brazil.

rome_analytics_quotes: Zach vaults to the top, roasting Gayden's hopes like last year's turkey.
Rafe drafted three countries that didn't qualify, yet he's ranked second. Draft day genius.
Devin's getting smoked by a teenager—two losses to Rafe already, that's brutal.
Sim's MVP is chaos agent Rafe, making algorithms sweat while knowing zilch.
